concerns.text: FR-007 mandates a sensitivity analysis sweeping timing boundaries over {0.01, 0.05, 0.1} absolute diff to report 'false-positive rates'. The idea asks for the effect of timing on performance, not a methodological validation of the cutoffs. Calculating 'false-positive rates' requires a ground-truth null distribution or simulation not present in the observational OULAD data. This appears to be over-reach (engineering gold-plating) or a methodological impossibility without a simulation step not requested in the idea.

User Story 2 defines 'Variable' as a third group based on 'median interval' but the binning logic in FR-004 and acceptance scenarios only explicitly define 'Immediate' (<2h) and 'Delayed' (2h–48h). The spec fails to define the boundary or logic for the 'Variable' group (e.g., >48h?), creating an ambiguity in the predictor variable definition.

User Story 1 and FR-001 require downloading OULAD and filtering for 'assessment' and 'forum' events. The idea asks for 'course completion rates' as an outcome. The spec does not explicitly require extracting the 'completion' status (e.g., pass/fail/withdraw) from the dataset, only 'final grade'. If 'completion' is a distinct binary variable in OULAD separate from the grade, this requirement is under-reach.
